text.title: Flag Killswitch Decision Criteria Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Flag Killswitch Decision Criteria must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Decision is logged with: option chosen, options rejected, evidence cited, expected reversal trigger. Reversal-trigger is mandatory.
rule.rationale: undocumented reversals erode trust faster than wrong decisions.

rule.statement: Each option carries its kill-criteria (numeric threshold at which it would be re-opened). 'We'll know when we see it' is rejected.
rule.rationale: without explicit kill-criteria, options drift permanently.

rule.statement: The decision is signed by the named decider. No anonymous, committee-by-default approvals.
The output names a single accountable owner; plural pronouns ('team', 'we') are rejected.
Output carries semver version and last_reviewed ISO date; records stale >90 days are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; versioning forces refresh.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md 'Applies If' do not all hold, skip this methodology and route to the correct one; do not retro-fit inputs.
rule.rationale: Forcing a methodology onto a misaligned task produces artefacts downstream consumers cannot trust.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Feature flag rollout decision (weekly) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
